content.fact: Force sensitivity does not make a person a trained Jedi or Sith; doctrine, training, discipline, and institutions matter.
content.injection: Do not treat every Force-sensitive character as a trained Jedi or Sith. Distinguish raw sensitivity from practiced techniques, rank, doctrine, and institutional membership.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: Reliable Force use generally depends on practice, emotional control, and instruction, even for unusually gifted characters.
content.injection: Portray controlled Force use as trained discipline. Gifted characters may have flashes of instinct, but reliable techniques need practice, focus, and limits.

content.fact: The Jedi Order is a religious, philosophical, and peacekeeping institution, not a species or generic synonym for anyone who uses the Force.
content.injection: Use Jedi to mean membership, training, or tradition within the Jedi Order. Do not use it as a blanket label for every light-side or Force-sensitive person.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.

content.fact: Traditional Jedi doctrine emphasizes restraint, service, emotional discipline, and defense over domination or revenge.
content.injection: When writing orthodox Jedi, bias them toward restraint, defense, negotiation, and self-control unless the character or era gives a reason to deviate.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.

entries.title: Jedi Tied to Republic Before Purge

context.label: Prequel and Clone Wars broad window

content.fact: Before Order 66, the Jedi Order is closely tied to the Galactic Republic and its institutions, especially during the Clone Wars.
content.injection: In prequel-era scenes, treat the Jedi as official Republic peacekeepers and commanders, not detached wandering adventurers unless the scene is outside institutional reach.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Prequel and Clone Wars broad window.
Respect story-position bounds from Episode I: The Phantom Menace to Episode III: Revenge of the Sith.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
content.notes: Generated development draft; verify during Saga review.

context.label: After Revenge of the Sith, before Return of the Jedi

content.fact: After Order 66, the Jedi Order is shattered; surviving Jedi are hidden, hunted, isolated, or operating under exceptional circumstances.
content.injection: After Revenge of the Sith, do not portray open Jedi institutions or routine Jedi public authority unless a narrower deck establishes a survivor enclave or later Luke-era restoration. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: After Revenge of the Sith, before Return of the Jedi.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

content.fact: Luke Skywalker has not rebuilt a functioning Jedi Order before the post-Endor Legends era.
content.injection: Before the post-Endor era, keep Luke’s Jedi status personal and developing. Do not introduce a rebuilt Jedi academy or new Jedi Order unless the story explicitly diverges. Use this as a background rule only; narrower active era/source cards should override it.

content.fact: Force visions can be symbolic, partial, misleading, or shaped by fear; they are not clean omniscient spoilers by default.
content.injection: Treat Force visions as unstable signals. They may warn, tempt, or foreshadow, but should not provide complete future-canon certainty unless the story specifically establishes that.

content.fact: Jedi mind influence is not universal mind control; it works best on weak-minded or susceptible targets and can fail against discipline, species resistance, or strong will.
content.injection: Use mind tricks as limited influence, not automatic domination. Let trained, strong-willed, resistant, or alert targets plausibly resist.

content.injection: Constrain Force pushes, pulls, throws, and lifts by attention, training, target size, resistance, and scene pressure.

entries.title: Force Lightning Is Advanced Dark Side

content.fact: Force lightning is an advanced dark-side expression, not a casual neutral technique available to ordinary Force users.
content.injection: Reserve Force lightning for trained dark-side users or exceptional circumstances established by the active story and source deck.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.

entries.title: Force Healing Is Not a Casual Reset

content.fact: Legends contains Force healing traditions, but healing is limited by training, injury severity, time, exhaustion, and the source tradition involved.
content.injection: Do not use Force healing as a consequence-free reset. Require training, limits, recovery time, or a specific technique introduced by the loaded deck.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Broad Star Wars Legends continuity.
Keep retrieval topic/entity-gated; this wide-context card should not flood unrelated scenes.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
